summary: First iteration. Backend (8/8 pytest) and Frontend (all 8 features) tested end-to-end against the Patrycja & Adrian wedding site. All critical flows working: API greeting, RSVP create (yes/no), validation 422s, GET list (no _id), stats; Hero, Countdown, Schedule interactivity, FAQ accordion, RSVP toggle/submit (POST 200 + success state visible in PL: 'Dziękujemy! Do zobaczenia 19 lipca.'), Kahoot QR (rendered as SVG with paths), nav anchor scroll.

issues: FAQ button has no aria-expanded attribute — works visually but lacks a11y semantics. Recommend adding aria-expanded and aria-controls for screen-reader support.

action_items: (Optional / minor) Add aria-expanded toggling on FAQ buttons for accessibility.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: created_at is stored as ISO string in Mongo and re-parsed on GET; consider storing as native BSON datetime to leverage indexing/sorting natively (current sort on string ISO works because lexicographic order matches chronological for UTC ISO).
server.py: no rate limiting / duplicate-prevention on POST /api/rsvp — anyone could spam submissions; acceptable for MVP.
Backend uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown'); migrate to lifespan handler in future cleanup.
FAQ component should toggle aria-expanded for a11y.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth on this site
seed_data_creation: Created 4 RSVPs prefixed TEST_ during pytest+UI runs (TEST_Anna Kowalska, TEST_Marek Nowak, TEST_UI Anna). Safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_rsvp_api.py and run cleanly with `pytest backend/tests/ -v`. All RSVP API contract endpoints validated (root, POST /api/rsvp yes+no, validation 422s, GET list w/o _id, GET stats). Frontend smoke covered: hero-title, countdown-section, schedule-item-N click updates schedule-detail, faq-item-N expands, rsvp form (toggle yes/no hides guests+dietary, submit -> rsvp-success), kahoot-qr is rendered as a top-level SVG (data-testid is on the svg root, so inner_html shows <path> children — that is correct and not a bug).
